name: Government AI Readiness Index 2025
coverage: 195 governments in the attached 2025 data workbook.
sourceName: Oxford Insights - Government AI Readiness Index 2025
caveat: Readiness score is a comparative policy-capacity indicator, not a legal-status finding or evidence of binding AI duties.
verificationNotes: Imported from the attached Oxford Insights 2025 XLSX; source URL points to the issuer page for the index and methodology.

name: AI and Democratic Values Index 2026
publisher: Center for AI and Digital Policy
coverage: 90 countries in the attached CAIDP 2026 CSV export.
sourceName: CAIDP - AI and Democratic Values Index 2026
caveat: CAIDP scores are a civil-society democratic-values assessment and should not be read as official legal status or treaty participation.
verificationNotes: Imported from the attached CAIDP 2026 CSV export; source URL points to the issuer report page.

name: UNESCO AI Ethics Readiness Assessment Methodology country hub
coverage: Countries listed by UNESCO as in preparation, in process, completed, or with a country profile on the Global Hub.
sourceName: UNESCO - Global AI Ethics and Governance Observatory Global Hub
caveat: RAM status indicates UNESCO assessment/profile activity; it is not a comparable numeric readiness score and not a national legal obligation.
verificationNotes: Parsed from the official UNESCO Global Hub country table and profile cards; subnational entries are listed in the review note rather than mapped as country-level records.

name: Stanford Global AI Vibrancy Tool public-data score
coverage: 66 countries in the official Global AI Vibrancy Tool full_data_09.24.25.csv public dataset.
sourceName: Stanford HAI - Global AI Vibrancy Tool
caveat: The score is contextual AI ecosystem evidence from the public tool data; it is not a legal-status or compliance score. Public country data vintage is 2024 even though the 2026 AI Index references the tool.
verificationNotes: Computed from the official Global AI Vibrancy Tool CSV, codebook, metric weights, and group weights exposed by the public tool bundle.

name: IMF AI Preparedness Index
coverage: Official IMF DataMapper AI Preparedness Index source family; country rows are not imported in this pass because the public page blocked automated data retrieval.
caveat: AI preparedness is a macro-context indicator. It is not legal status, treaty participation, or evidence of binding AI duties.
verificationNotes: Official IMF DataMapper source identified; automated fetch returned an access-control page, so this source is tracked without country score rows until a durable data feed is verified.

name: OECD AI Observatory Index 2026
coverage: Official OECD iLibrary source family for AI Observatory index evidence; country rows are source-only until the report data is normalized.
caveat: OECD index evidence is policy and ecosystem context. It does not establish legal effect or national-law coverage.
verificationNotes: Tracked as a source family for future normalized rows; no country score rows are imported in this pass.

name: OECD.AI policy observatory
coverage: Official OECD.AI policy source family for national AI policy initiatives and observatory context.
caveat: Policy-observatory entries are context and discovery leads; they are not treated as legal status unless the underlying official national source is separately verified.
verificationNotes: Tracked as a source family for future source-delta monitoring and policy database reconciliation.

name: Global Index on Responsible AI
coverage: Issuer source family for responsible-AI governance indicators; country rows are not imported until methodology and data are normalized into this app schema.
caveat: Responsible-AI index scores are civil-society context indicators and do not establish binding legal duties or official compliance status.
verificationNotes: Tracked as a source family only. Import country rows after a transparent methodology and machine-readable data crosswalk are reviewed.

name: Netherlands public algorithm register
coverage: Issuer-controlled example source for public-sector algorithm and AI transparency registry practice.
sourceName: Government of the Netherlands - Algorithm Register
caveat: Public-sector register evidence shows transparency infrastructure, not a general national AI-law score or proof of private-sector obligations.
verificationNotes: Tracked as source-only context for future public-sector AI registry expansion.

name: International Network of AI Safety Institutes
coverage: Official network source family for AI safety institute coordination and technical-governance cooperation.
sourceName: NIST - International Network of AI Safety Institutes
caveat: Safety-institute coordination is governance context. It is not a treaty, national law, or binding company obligation unless a separate legal source says so.
verificationNotes: Tracked as source-only context for future safety-institute and technical-governance expansion.

name: European Commission AI Factories
coverage: Official EU source family for AI Factories, EuroHPC infrastructure, and EU compute-investment signals.
caveat: AI Factories indicate infrastructure and compute-capacity context. They are not national AI laws, treaty participation, or binding obligations by themselves.
verificationNotes: Official European Commission source identified for future EU compute/infrastructure normalization; country rows are not imported in this pass.

name: U.S. National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource pilot
coverage: Official NSF source family for U.S. shared AI research infrastructure and compute-resource access.
sourceName: NSF - National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource Pilot
caveat: NAIRR is research-infrastructure context. It does not establish private-sector AI duties or binding AI-law coverage.
verificationNotes: Official NSF source identified for future compute/infrastructure normalization; country rows are not imported in this pass.

name: UK Guidelines for AI procurement
coverage: Official GOV.UK source family for public-sector AI procurement guidance.
caveat: Procurement guidance is public-sector practice context. It should not be treated as a general binding AI statute or as private-sector legal coverage.
verificationNotes: Official GOV.UK procurement guidance source identified for future public-procurement normalization; no country score rows are imported in this pass.
